  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which strengthening focus should we apply to this passage? A) Style-only polish: tighten sentences for clarity and punch without altering claims or structure; B) Positioning: reframe the thesis and contribution, potentially reordering content and adjusting the strength/placement of ‘first’ claims; C) Claim rigor: check and calibrate the strength of claims against the stated results (and temper overclaims), keeping structure largely unchanged.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening may require sharpening the thesis and contribution framing so the core claim and gap are clearer and more impactful.",
    "R1.2": "Ensuring the internal argument from market participation requirements to the constrained RL approach is logically tight enhances persuasiveness.",
    "R1.3": "Making the passage stronger depends on claims being tightly aligned with the reported results and numbers in the text.",
    "R1.4": "Because the text asserts ‘first’ contributions, assessing and calibrating novelty versus prior work is central to credible strengthening.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level clarity, concision, and register directly affect perceived strength and readability of the passage.",
    "RX.1": "Clear qualifiers (e.g., ‘to our knowledge,’ ‘in some markets’) should be used and maintained to disclose uncertainty appropriately.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overclaim is critical when asserting novelty and general performance to maintain credibility.",
    "RX.3": "We must confine any strengthening to this passage and the chosen focus rather than expanding scope or adding unsupported content.",
    "RX.4": "Overstating commitment reliability or market compliance could mislead practitioners and create downstream risks.",
    "RX.5": "Any edits must preserve traceability to the original claims and results so readers can verify provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which strengthening focus should we apply to this passage? A) Style-only polish: tighten sentences for clarity and punch without altering claims or structure; B) Positioning: reframe the thesis and contribution, potentially reordering content and adjusting the strength/placement of ‘first’ claims; C) Claim rigor: check and calibrate the strength of claims against the stated results (and temper overclaims), keeping structure largely unchanged.

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening may require sharpening the thesis and contribution framing so the core claim and gap are clearer and more impactful.
rationale.R1.2: Ensuring the internal argument from market participation requirements to the constrained RL approach is logically tight enhances persuasiveness.
rationale.R1.3: Making the passage stronger depends on claims being tightly aligned with the reported results and numbers in the text.
rationale.R1.4: Because the text asserts ‘first’ contributions, assessing and calibrating novelty versus prior work is central to credible strengthening.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level clarity, concision, and register directly affect perceived strength and readability of the passage.
rationale.RX.1: Clear qualifiers (e.g., ‘to our knowledge,’ ‘in some markets’) should be used and maintained to disclose uncertainty appropriately.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaim is critical when asserting novelty and general performance to maintain credibility.
rationale.RX.3: We must confine any strengthening to this passage and the chosen focus rather than expanding scope or adding unsupported content.
rationale.RX.4: Overstating commitment reliability or market compliance could mislead practitioners and create downstream risks.
rationale.RX.5: Any edits must preserve traceability to the original claims and results so readers can verify provenance.
